tool_arguments_json.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Add the durable runtime context foundation for pinned entities and workspace/run identity without disturbing capture/archive storage.

## Motivation ("why")

A transcript does not preserve active attention state in a compact, mutable form. Agents need to resume the same logical workspace, retain selected entities, and start a distinct linked run without replaying prior turns.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on idempotent workspace initialization/resume, durable pinned entity URNs, distinct linked run IDs, immediate file-backed persistence, headers-only views, and optional non-blocking feedback emission. Once a workspace is finished, ordinary init is a byte-stable read and new run lineage is rejected.

## Guards

- `val-session-bootstrap-schema-validation`.
- `val-session-init-idempotency`.
- `val-session-context-capture-isolation`.
- `val-session-run-lineage`.
- `val-session-workflow-finish`.

## Positions

- Existing capture models: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs`.
- Existing capture persistence: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`.
- Runtime context model and operations: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs` and `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`; validated by `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`, `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`, `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714`, and the latest `val-session-workflow-finish` execution.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- `workspace_session_id` identifies a durable logical workspace.
- Every agent execution has a distinct `run_id` and optional `predecessor_run_id`.
- Initialization is load-or-create and never clobbers pins or lineage.
- Finished-workspace ordinary init returns persisted context without updating timestamps or rewriting `context.json` or `active_workspace_session.json`; force-new-run init and resume reject.
- Init, resume, finish, and runtime mutations serialize under one OS-held exclusive workspace lock; lock age alone never permits reclamation.
- Pinned tickets, specs, and rules are cross-store URNs with relation/reason metadata.
- Mutations flush before returning within the platform guarantees declared by the workflow spec.
- Read/view returns short headers and metadata, never full entity bodies.
- Duplicate pin and missing unpin are idempotent no-ops.
- Feedback usage emission is optional through an injected sink; sink absence or failure cannot corrupt or reject a successful pin.
- Existing capture manifests and transcripts remain byte-identical when context mutates, and context remains byte-identical when capture persists.

# Non-goals

- Workflow graph persistence, rendering, handoff, and finish, owned by `c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.
- Cascade auto-pinning.
- CLI/MCP transport.
- Full-body entity resolution.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Fresh initialization creates durable context with workspace ID, schema version, timestamps, empty pins, and initial run lineage.
2. Resume preserves pins and adds a distinct linked run without reusing the outgoing run ID before finish; resume rejects after finish.
3. Plain init after finish is read-only and leaves runtime context and active-workspace files byte-identical.
4. Pin/unpin is idempotent and immediately persistent.
5. Headers-only view never includes full bodies.

<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Add the durable runtime context foundation for pinned entities and workspace/run identity without disturbing capture/archive storage.

## Motivation ("why")

A transcript does not preserve active attention state in a compact, mutable form. Agents need to resume the same logical workspace, retain selected entities, and start a distinct linked run without replaying prior turns.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on idempotent workspace initialization/resume, durable pinned entity URNs, distinct linked run IDs, immediate file-backed persistence, headers-only views, and optional non-blocking feedback emission. Once a workspace is finished, ordinary init is a byte-stable read and new run lineage is rejected.

## Guards

- `val-session-bootstrap-schema-validation`.
- `val-session-init-idempotency`.
- `val-session-context-capture-isolation`.
- `val-session-run-lineage`.
- `val-session-workflow-finish`.

## Positions

- Existing capture models: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs`.
- Existing capture persistence: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`.
- Runtime context model and operations: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs` and `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`; validated by `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`, `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`, `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714`, and the latest `val-session-workflow-finish` execution.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- `workspace_session_id` identifies a durable logical workspace.
- Every agent execution has a distinct `run_id` and optional `predecessor_run_id`.
- Initialization is load-or-create and never clobbers pins or lineage.
- Finished-workspace ordinary init returns persisted context without updating timestamps or rewriting `context.json` or `active_workspace_session.json`; force-new-run init and resume reject.
- Init, resume, finish, and runtime mutations serialize under one OS-held exclusive workspace lock; lock age alone never permits reclamation.
- Pinned tickets, specs, and rules are cross-store URNs with relation/reason metadata.
- Mutations flush before returning within the platform guarantees declared by the workflow spec.
- Read/view returns short headers and metadata, never full entity bodies.
- Duplicate pin and missing unpin are idempotent no-ops.
- Feedback usage emission is optional through an injected sink; sink absence or failure cannot corrupt or reject a successful pin.
- Existing capture manifests and transcripts remain byte-identical when context mutates, and context remains byte-identical when capture persists.

# Non-goals

- Workflow graph persistence, rendering, handoff, and finish, owned by `c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.
- Cascade auto-pinning.
- CLI/MCP transport.
- Full-body entity resolution.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Fresh initialization creates durable context with workspace ID, schema version, timestamps, empty pins, and initial run lineage.
2. Resume preserves pins and adds a distinct linked run without reusing the outgoing run ID before finish; resume rejects after finish.
3. Plain init after finish is read-only and leaves runtime context and active-workspace files byte-identical.
4. Pin/unpin is idempotent and immediately persistent.
5. Headers-only view never includes full bodies.
6. Optional feedback sink receives successful pin usage when configured and cannot block persistence.
7. Capture/context byte-isolation regressions pass.
8. Focused `cargo test -p session-api` coverage validates the contract.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Implementation ticket: `412964a3-e1c3-47da-94ad-268ff20441c0`.
- Remediation ticket: `6b1edff1-bc32-40c7-b3a9-fb1292b0213f`.
- Downstream workflow ticket: `70cd7056-c342-4433-ad60-5bc798f61aa6`.
- Downstream transport ticket: `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.

## Dependent expectation

Once a workspace is finished, ordinary init is a byte-stable read and new run lineage is rejected.

## Guards

- `val-session-bootstrap-schema-validation`.
- `val-session-init-idempotency`.
- `val-session-context-capture-isolation`.

- Runtime context model and operations: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs` and `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`; validated by `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`, `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`, `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714`, and the latest `val-session-workflow-finish` execution.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Every agent execution has a distinct `run_id` and optional `predecessor_run_id`.

- Finished-workspace ordinary init returns persisted context without updating timestamps or rewriting `context.json` or `active_workspace_session.json`; force-new-run init and resume reject.
- Init, resume, finish, and runtime mutations serialize under one OS-held exclusive workspace lock; lock age alone never permits reclamation.
- Pinned tickets, specs, and rules are cross-store URNs with relation/reason metadata.
- Mutations flush before returning within the platform guarantees declared by the workflow spec.
- Read/view returns short headers and metadata, never full entity bodies.
- Duplicate pin and missing unpin are idempotent no-ops.
- Feedback usage emission is optional through an injected sink; sink absence or failure cannot corrupt or reject a successful pin.

# Acceptance Criteria

7. Capture/context byte-isolation regressions pass.

field_map.implementation_summary: Remediation implementation is complete and ready for independent review. Runtime locking now uses the standard library's cross-platform OS-held exclusive file lock with a stable lock file: lock age never permits theft, process exit releases ownership, and guard drop does not unlink a successor's lock inode. Ordinary init after finish returns persisted state without rewriting context.json or active_workspace_session.json; new-run init and resume still reject. Deterministic finish-versus-mutation/init/resume coverage and Windows replacement-failure preservation coverage were added. The parent, runtime-context, and durable-workflow specs now limit durability claims to verified behavior; Unix parent-directory sync errors propagate and no unproven Windows replace-existing or power-loss guarantee remains. Action 8 remains blocked pending independent review.
field_map.validation_summary: Authoritative evidence: exec-val-session-workflow-finish-remediation-20260714 under val-session-workflow-finish. Windows results: cargo test -p session-api passed 106 tests; cargo test -p session-api -p session-cli -p session-mcp passed 124 tests; cargo fmt --check -p session-api passed. Focused regressions passed for aged live lock exclusion/ownership-safe release, finished-init byte stability, deterministic finish exclusion of mutation/init/resume, and failed Windows replacement preserving previous bytes. Workspace-wide cargo fmt --check remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside session-api.

data_json.content: The durable graph now shows all five remediation nodes done and `action-8` still pending. I’m updating the ticket’s implementation and validation summaries, then I’ll run ticket/spec evidence health and inspect only the resulting changed-file set before releasing ownership; ticket closure will remain blocked on genuinely independent review.

data_json.content:
**Verdict**
No blocking findings. Green light granted.

The remediation fixes the prior defects at their source:

- store.rs serializes init with runtime mutations and returns finished ordinary init before either persistence call.
- store.rs uses an OS-held file lock, with no age-based reclamation or lock-file unlinking.
- store_tests.rs verifies aged-lock exclusion and stable successor ownership.
- store_tests.rs creates a genuine finish-versus-mutation/init/resume interleaving.
- store_tests.rs verifies finished ordinary init is byte-stable.
- store_helpers.rs now states only the durability guarantees the implementation actually establishes.

The three specs consistently reflect those narrower guarantees, and exec-val-session-workflow-finish-remediation-20260714 is discoverable through test-api with the correct run, ticket, specs, acceptance criteria, and passing outcome.

**Residual Risk**
The lock regression uses separate handles in one process rather than a spawned competing process. That still exercises the standard library’s OS lock behavior and is sufficient for this remediation; a process-exit/crash integration test would be defense-in-depth, not an action-8 blocker.

Failed renames can also leave UUID temporary files behind. That is minor cleanup debt, not a state-corruption issue: the previous destination remains intact.

**Next State**
Ticket 6b1edff1 Authoritative finish, live ticket state, atomic durability, and CLI contract is ready to transition from `in-review` to `done`.

Ticket b4a8dc5e Minimal bootstrapper + selective instruction loading remains formally blocked only because that lifecycle transition has not happened yet. Close the remediation ticket, then proceed with action 8.

arguments.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Replace the always-on generated instruction load with one minimal bootstrapper plus discoverable, pinnable rule entries that are rendered into a focused per-session instruction set.

## Motivation ("why")

Universal `applyTo: "**"` instruction files inject unrelated ticket, spec, commit, and token-efficiency guidance into every task. The canonical rule store already makes those bodies searchable; sessions need a small discovery contract and an explicit render path instead of unconditional expansion.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on only the minimal bootstrapper being universal, the removed instruction bodies remaining searchable in rule-api, and a session rendering exactly its pinned rule entries without expanding unrelated rules.

## Guards

- `val-session-selective-rule-render`.
- `val-rule-selective-instruction-generation`.
- `val-rule-sync-targets`.

## Positions

- Canonical searchable rule entries: `implemented` at `.rule/rules/` and `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/store/store_query.rs`.
- Generated instruction target selection: `partial` at `rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml`; four large instruction targets are still generated.
- Minimal universal bootstrapper: `not-implemented`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.
- Session-side pinned-rule rendering: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Exactly one generated bootstrapper instruction uses `applyTo: "**"`.
- The bootstrapper stays below 500 tokens and directs an agent to initialize/resume session context, search rules, pin relevant rule URNs, and render the pinned instruction set.
- `ticket-system.instructions.md`, `commit.instructions.md`, `spec-system.instructions.md`, and `token-efficiency.instructions.md` are not generated into the always-on instruction surface.
- Their canonical `.rule` entries remain indexed and discoverable through representative rule searches.
- Session rendering accepts the current workspace session ID, resolves only pinned rule URNs from the sibling rule store, preserves canonical deterministic ordering, and returns generated markdown containing only those rule bodies.
- Non-rule pins never enter the rendered instruction set. Missing, malformed, cross-workspace, or non-rule references fail explicitly rather than broadening the render.
- Rule target generation remains deterministic and `rule sync-targets --check` passes.

# Non-goals

- Changing the canonical rule manifest or search index formats.
- Automatically selecting rules from vague semantic matches.
- Rendering ticket or spec bodies into the session instruction set.
- Removing narrowly scoped instruction files for concrete code paths.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Generated always-on instructions contain only the sub-500-token bootstrapper; the four converted large files are absent.
2. Representative searches find canonical ticket-state, commit-hook, spec-authoring, and token-efficiency rule entries.
3. Spec-system guidance is not duplicated in generated output.
4. `rule sync-targets --check` is deterministic on the reduced target set.
5. Given pinned rule entries plus unrelated pins, session rendering emits exactly the pinned rule bodies in deterministic order.
6. Missing or invalid pinned rule references fail explicitly without rendering unrelated content.
7. Session CLI and MCP expose the focused render operation consistently.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Implementation ticket: `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.
- Pin/view dependency: `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.
- Design dependency: `afa00b5c-c736-4d75-b157-d3e9ce90d819`.

data_json.content: The owning path is clear: target reduction belongs in `rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml`, while focused per-session composition belongs in `session-api` using existing `rule-api` storage/rendering. The discriminating check will pin two rule URNs and assert the rendered instruction set contains exactly those two bodies; generation checks will assert only the bootstrapper remains universal and the four large targets disappear. Before editing, I’m updating the spec to that concrete v2 contract and transitioning/checking in the ticket.

Universal `applyTo: "**"` instruction files inject unrelated ticket, spec, commit, and token-efficiency guidance into every task. The canonical rule store already makes those bodies searchable; sessions need a small discovery contract and an explicit render path instead of unconditional expansion.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on only the minimal bootstrapper being universal, the removed instruction bodies remaining searchable in rule-api, and a session rendering exactly its pinned rule entries without expanding unrelated rules.

## Guards

- `val-session-selective-rule-render`.
- `val-rule-selective-instruction-generation`.
- `val-rule-sync-targets`.

## Positions

- Canonical searchable rule entries: `implemented` at `.rule/rules/` and `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/store/store_query.rs`.
- Generated instruction target selection: `partial` at `rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml`; four large instruction targets are still generated.
- Minimal universal bootstrapper: `not-implemented`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.
- Session-side pinned-rule rendering: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Exactly one generated bootstrapper instruction uses `applyTo: "**"`.
- The bootstrapper stays below 500 tokens and directs an agent to initialize/resume session context, search rules, pin relevant rule URNs, and render the pinned instruction set.
- `ticket-system.instructions.md`, `commit.instructions.md`, `spec-system.instructions.md`, and `token-efficiency.instructions.md` are not generated into the always-on instruction surface.
- Their canonical `.rule` entries remain indexed and discoverable through representative rule searches.
- Session rendering accepts the current workspace session ID, resolves only pinned rule URNs from the sibling rule store, preserves canonical deterministic ordering, and returns generated markdown containing only those rule bodies.
- Non-rule pins never enter the rendered instruction set. Missing, malformed, cross-workspace, or non-rule references fail explicitly rather than broadening the render.
- Rule target generation remains deterministic and `rule sync-targets --check` passes.

# Non-goals

- Changing the canonical rule manifest or search index formats.
- Automatically selecting rules from vague semantic matches.
- Rendering ticket or spec bodies into the session instruction set.
- Removing narrowly scoped instruction files for concrete code paths.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Generated always-on instructions contain only the sub-500-token bootstrapper; the four converted large files are absent.
2. Representative searches find canonical ticket-state, commit-hook, spec-authoring, and token-efficiency rule entries.
3. Spec-system guidance is not duplicated in generated output.
4. `rule sync-targets --check` is deterministic on the reduced target set.
5. Given pinned rule entries plus unrelated pins, session rendering emits exactly the pinned rule bodies in deterministic order.
6. Missing or invalid pinned rule references fail explicitly without rendering unrelated content.
7. Session CLI and MCP expose the focused render operation consistently.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Implementation ticket: `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.
- Pin/view dependency: `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.
- Design dependency: `afa00b5c-c736-4d75-b157-d3e9ce90d819`.

arguments.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Replace the always-on generated instruction load with one minimal bootstrapper plus discoverable, pinnable rule entries that are rendered into a focused per-session instruction set.

## Motivation ("why")

Universal `applyTo: "**"` instruction files inject unrelated ticket, spec, commit, and token-efficiency guidance into every task. The canonical rule store already makes those bodies searchable; sessions need a small discovery contract and an explicit render path instead of unconditional expansion.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on only the minimal bootstrapper being universal, the large instruction bodies remaining searchable and available on demand, and a session rendering exactly its pinned rule entries without expanding unrelated rules.

## Guards

- `val-session-selective-rule-render`.
- `val-rule-selective-instruction-generation`.
- `val-rule-sync-targets`.

## Positions

- Canonical searchable rule entries: `implemented` at `.rule/rules/` and `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/store/store_query.rs`.
- Generated instruction target selection: `partial` at `rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml` and `rule-targets/55-session-optimization.yaml`; ticket/spec/commit are still universal while token-efficiency is narrowly scoped.
- Minimal universal bootstrapper: `not-implemented`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.
- Session-side pinned-rule rendering: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Exactly one generated bootstrapper instruction uses `applyTo: "**"`.
- The bootstrapper stays below 500 tokens and directs an agent to initialize/resume session context, search rules, pin relevant rule URNs, and render the pinned instruction set.
- `ticket-system.instructions.md`, `commit.instructions.md`, and `spec-system.instructions.md` remain deterministic generated artifacts with description-only discovery and no universal `applyTo`; `token-efficiency.instructions.md` retains only its narrow file globs.
- Their canonical `.rule` entries remain indexed and discoverable through representative rule searches.
- Session rendering accepts the current workspace session ID, resolves only pinned rule URNs from the sibling rule store, preserves canonical deterministic ordering, and returns generated markdown containing only those rule bodies.
- Non-rule pins never enter the rendered instruction set. Missing, malformed, cross-workspace, or non-rule references fail explicitly rather than broadening the render.
- Rule target generation remains deterministic and `rule sync-targets --check` passes.

# Non-goals

- Changing the canonical rule manifest or search index formats.
- Automatically selecting rules from vague semantic matches.
- Rendering ticket or spec bodies into the session instruction set.
- Removing narrowly scoped instruction files for concrete code paths.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. The generated always-on instruction set contains only the sub-500-token bootstrapper; the four large bodies are absent from that set.
2. Representative searches find canonical ticket-state, commit-hook, spec-authoring, and token-efficiency rule entries.
3. Spec-system guidance appears exactly once in its generated on-demand artifact.
4. `rule sync-targets --check` is deterministic on the reduced universal set.
5. Given pinned rule entries plus unrelated pins, session rendering emits exactly the pinned rule bodies in deterministic order.
6. Missing or invalid pinned rule references fail explicitly without rendering unrelated content.
7. Session CLI and MCP expose the focused render operation consistently.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Implementation ticket: `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.
- Pin/view dependency: `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.
- Design dependency: `afa00b5c-c736-4d75-b157-d3e9ce90d819`.

data_json.content: The existing customization mechanism gives a smaller, safer implementation than deleting target blocks: instruction files with a `description` and no `applyTo` remain discoverable on demand but are not force-loaded. I’m preserving their deterministic generated artifacts, removing universal `applyTo` from ticket/spec/commit, keeping token-efficiency’s existing narrow scope, and adding one universal bootstrapper; I’m also correcting the spec wording from “files absent” to “bodies absent from the always-on set.”

# Summary

Replace the always-on generated instruction load with one minimal bootstrapper plus discoverable, pinnable rule entries that are rendered into a focused per-session instruction set.

## Motivation ("why")

Universal `applyTo: "**"` instruction files inject unrelated ticket, spec, commit, and token-efficiency guidance into every task. The canonical rule store already makes those bodies searchable; sessions need a small discovery contract and an explicit render path instead of unconditional expansion.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on only the minimal bootstrapper being universal, the large instruction bodies remaining searchable and available on demand, and a session rendering exactly its pinned rule entries without expanding unrelated rules.

## Guards

- `val-session-selective-rule-render`.
- `val-rule-selective-instruction-generation`.
- `val-rule-sync-targets`.

## Positions

- Canonical searchable rule entries: `implemented` at `.rule/rules/` and `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/store/store_query.rs`.
- Generated instruction target selection: `partial` at `rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml` and `rule-targets/55-session-optimization.yaml`; ticket/spec/commit are still universal while token-efficiency is narrowly scoped.
- Minimal universal bootstrapper: `not-implemented`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.
- Session-side pinned-rule rendering: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Exactly one generated bootstrapper instruction uses `applyTo: "**"`.
- The bootstrapper stays below 500 tokens and directs an agent to initialize/resume session context, search rules, pin relevant rule URNs, and render the pinned instruction set.
- `ticket-system.instructions.md`, `commit.instructions.md`, and `spec-system.instructions.md` remain deterministic generated artifacts with description-only discovery and no universal `applyTo`; `token-efficiency.instructions.md` retains only its narrow file globs.
- Their canonical `.rule` entries remain indexed and discoverable through representative rule searches.
- Session rendering accepts the current workspace session ID, resolves only pinned rule URNs from the sibling rule store, preserves canonical deterministic ordering, and returns generated markdown containing only those rule bodies.
- Non-rule pins never enter the rendered instruction set. Missing, malformed, cross-workspace, or non-rule references fail explicitly rather than broadening the render.
- Rule target generation remains deterministic and `rule sync-targets --check` passes.

# Non-goals

- Changing the canonical rule manifest or search index formats.
- Automatically selecting rules from vague semantic matches.
- Rendering ticket or spec bodies into the session instruction set.
- Removing narrowly scoped instruction files for concrete code paths.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. The generated always-on instruction set contains only the sub-500-token bootstrapper; the four large bodies are absent from that set.
2. Representative searches find canonical ticket-state, commit-hook, spec-authoring, and token-efficiency rule entries.
3. Spec-system guidance appears exactly once in its generated on-demand artifact.
4. `rule sync-targets --check` is deterministic on the reduced universal set.
5. Given pinned rule entries plus unrelated pins, session rendering emits exactly the pinned rule bodies in deterministic order.
6. Missing or invalid pinned rule references fail explicitly without rendering unrelated content.
7. Session CLI and MCP expose the focused render operation consistently.

# Traceability

# Summary

Replace the always-on generated instruction load with one minimal bootstrapper plus discoverable, pinnable rule entries that are rendered into a focused per-session instruction set.

## Motivation ("why")

Universal `applyTo: "**"` instruction files inject unrelated ticket, spec, commit, and token-efficiency guidance into every task. The canonical rule store already makes those bodies searchable; sessions need a small discovery contract and an explicit render path instead of unconditional expansion.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on only the minimal bootstrapper being universal, the large instruction bodies remaining searchable and available on demand, and a session rendering exactly its pinned rule entries without expanding unrelated rules.

## Guards

- `val-session-selective-rule-render`.
- `val-rule-selective-instruction-generation`.
- `val-rule-sync-targets`.

## Positions

- Canonical searchable rule entries: `implemented` at `.rule/rules/` and `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/store/store_query.rs`.
- Generated instruction target selection: `partial` at `rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml` and `rule-targets/55-session-optimization.yaml`; ticket/spec/commit are still universal while token-efficiency is narrowly scoped.
- Minimal universal bootstrapper: `not-implemented`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.
- Session-side pinned-rule rendering: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`; owned by ticket `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Exactly one generated bootstrapper instruction uses `applyTo: "**"`.
- The bootstrapper stays below 500 tokens and directs an agent to initialize/resume session context, search rules, pin relevant rule URNs, and render the pinned instruction set.
- `ticket-system.instructions.md`, `commit.instructions.md`, and `spec-system.instructions.md` remain deterministic generated artifacts with description-only discovery and no universal `applyTo`; `token-efficiency.instructions.md` retains only its narrow file globs.
- Their canonical `.rule` entries remain indexed and discoverable through representative rule searches.
- Session rendering accepts the current workspace session ID, resolves only pinned rule URNs from the sibling rule store, preserves canonical deterministic ordering, and returns generated markdown containing only those rule bodies.
- Non-rule pins never enter the rendered instruction set. Missing, malformed, cross-workspace, or non-rule references fail explicitly rather than broadening the render.
- Rule target generation remains deterministic and `rule sync-targets --check` passes.

# Non-goals

- Changing the canonical rule manifest or search index formats.
- Automatically selecting rules from vague semantic matches.
- Rendering ticket or spec bodies into the session instruction set.
- Removing narrowly scoped instruction files for concrete code paths.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. The generated always-on instruction set contains only the sub-500-token bootstrapper; the four large bodies are absent from that set.
2. Representative searches find canonical ticket-state, commit-hook, spec-authoring, and token-efficiency rule entries.
3. Spec-system guidance appears exactly once in its generated on-demand artifact.
4. `rule sync-targets --check` is deterministic on the reduced universal set.
5. Given pinned rule entries plus unrelated pins, session rendering emits exactly the pinned rule bodies in deterministic order.
6. Missing or invalid pinned rule references fail explicitly without rendering unrelated content.
7. Session CLI and MCP expose the focused render operation consistently.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Implementation ticket: `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`.

arguments.command:
rtk cargo run -p rule-cli --bin rule -- --workspace /c/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine --toon create --title "Session Bootstrap" --slug "context-engine/instructions/session-bootstrap" --file-kind ".instructions" --section "session-bootstrap-instructions" --repo context-engine --path-scope ".agents/instructions/session-bootstrap.instructions.md" --order-key 0 --body $'---
description: "Minimal session bootstrap for discovering and pinning task-relevant guidance."
applyTo: "**"
---

## Session Bootstrap

1. Initialize or resume the durable workspace with the session tools.
2. Search the rule store for guidance relevant to the current task; do not load broad instruction bodies by default.
3. Pin the selected rule URNs to the workspace session.
4. Render the pinned rule instruction set through the session render operation and follow only that focused guidance.
5. Keep ticket, spec, and validation authority in their owning stores.'
arguments.explanation: Create the canonical minimal bootstrap rule entry through rule-cli.

data_json.content: I’m creating the bootstrap as a canonical rule entry now. Its body is intentionally compact and names only the session and rule discovery flow; once the CLI returns the UUID, I’ll claim that rule directory and wire it into the generated target.

-- Keep specs focused on system properties, acceptance criteria, evidence, and non-goals.
-- Keep implementation plans, rollout sequencing, and execution notes in tickets unless they materially affect the contract.
-- Preserve traceability between specs, tickets, validation evidence, and neighboring specs.
-
-## Spec Quality — Standing Obligations
-
-These rules apply whenever spec work is involved, not only when editing spec-system code.
-
-### Orientation (start of every session)
-
-Before writing or editing a spec:
-
-- search existing specs for the behavior first
-- search related tickets so the spec can link the current execution plan
-- check whether a neighboring or parent spec already owns the requested slice
-
-Prefer `spec-mcp` and `ticket-mcp` tools when available. Fall back to `./target/debug/spec.exe` and `./target/debug/ticket.exe` when needed.
-
-### Discovery Before Creating
-
-Always search for an existing spec before creating a new one. Duplicate specs weaken the repository contract.
-
-Prefer updating a matching spec when:
-- the behavior belongs to the same component and scope
-- the existing spec can absorb the acceptance criteria without becoming unfocused
-- the requested change is a refinement rather than a new contract slice
-
-Create a new spec when:
-- the requested behavior is a distinct contract slice
-- the existing spec would become too broad or mix unrelated concerns
-- the new work needs its own acceptance criteria and evidence trail
-
-## Spec Authoring Workflow
-
-### Choose Component, Slug, and Parent
-
-- Use the owning subsystem or workflow area as the component.
-- Keep slugs lowercase, use `-` within segments, and `/` between segments.
-- Prefer a parent spec only when the new slice is clearly a child of an existing broader contract.
-- Avoid creating shallow duplicate siblings with overlapping goals.
-
-### Structure the Spec
-
-Each spec should make review straightforward. Capture, when relevant:
-- goal or intended behavior
-- problem or current-state gap
-- scope
-- non-goals
-- acceptance criteria
-- traceability or evidence expectations
-
-Acceptance criteria should be concrete enough that a reviewer can tell what evidence proves the work is done.
-
-### Link Tickets, Tests, and Related Specs
-
-Specs should explicitly link the work needed to satisfy or verify the contract.
-
-- Link the exact related ticket folder paths returned by ticket tools. Do not synthesize ticket paths.
-- Render ticket references per the Clickable Reference Policy in `AGENTS.md`.
-- Record the validation plan or completed validation results needed to evaluate the spec.
-- Link related specs when they define prerequisites, shared contracts, or adjacent behavior.
-- When docs or generated guidance are part of the deliverable, include them in the traceability or evidence section.
-
-Use a clear evidence vocabulary when possible, including validation commands, expected evidence objects, and blocked or passing results.
-
-### Validation Before Review
-
-Before moving spec work toward review, verify:
-- the acceptance criteria are testable
-- the linked tickets are sufficient to execute the work
-- the validation evidence is concrete, not implied
-- related specs are linked where cross-spec behavior matters
-- the spec still describes the contract, not a ticket-sized implementation plan
-
-## Workflow Expectations
-
-- When requirements, goals, or behavior change, create or update the relevant spec before implementation.
-- When implementation reveals a contract change, update the spec and its evidence trail immediately.
-- Keep ticket links, validation results, and related spec references current enough that another agent can continue the work without reconstructing intent.
-- Use the Spec Agent when work is primarily about creating or refining specs rather than implementing code.
-- If ambiguity remains after focused search, ask one concise clarification instead of guessing.

-- Prefer a parent spec only when the new slice is clearly a child of an existing broader contract.
-- Avoid creating shallow duplicate siblings with overlapping goals.
-
-### Structure the Spec
-
-Each spec should make review straightforward. Capture, when relevant:
-- goal or intended behavior
-- problem or current-state gap
-- scope
-- non-goals
-- acceptance criteria
-- traceability or evidence expectations
-
-Acceptance criteria should be concrete enough that a reviewer can tell what evidence proves the work is done.
-
-### Link Tickets, Tests, and Related Specs
-
-Specs should explicitly link the work needed to satisfy or verify the contract.
-
-- Link the exact related ticket folder paths returned by ticket tools. Do not synthesize ticket paths.
-- Render ticket references per the Clickable Reference Policy in `AGENTS.md`.
-- Record the validation plan or completed validation results needed to evaluate the spec.
-- Link related specs when they define prerequisites, shared contracts, or adjacent behavior.
-- When docs or generated guidance are part of the deliverable, include them in the traceability or evidence section.
-
-Use a clear evidence vocabulary when possible, including validation commands, expected evidence objects, and blocked or passing results.
-
-### Validation Before Review
-
-Before moving spec work toward review, verify:
-- the acceptance criteria are testable
-- the linked tickets are sufficient to execute the work
-- the validation evidence is concrete, not implied
-- related specs are linked where cross-spec behavior matters
-- the spec still describes the contract, not a ticket-sized implementation plan
-
-## Workflow Expectations
-
-- When requirements, goals, or behavior change, create or update the relevant spec before implementation.
-- When implementation reveals a contract change, update the spec and its evidence trail immediately.
-- Keep ticket links, validation results, and related spec references current enough that another agent can continue the work without reconstructing intent.
-- Use the Spec Agent when work is primarily about creating or refining specs rather than implementing code.
-- If ambiguity remains after focused search, ask one concise clarification instead of guessing.

arguments.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Replace the always-on generated instruction load with one minimal bootstrapper plus discoverable, pinnable rule entries that are rendered into a focused per-session instruction set.

## Motivation ("why")

Universal `applyTo: "**"` instruction files inject unrelated ticket, spec, commit, and token-efficiency guidance into every task. The canonical rule store already makes those bodies searchable; sessions need a small discovery contract and an explicit render path instead of unconditional expansion.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on only the minimal bootstrapper being universal, the large instruction bodies remaining searchable and available on demand, and a session rendering exactly its pinned rule entries without expanding unrelated rules.

## Guards

- `val-session-selective-rule-render`.
- `val-rule-selective-instruction-generation`.
- `val-rule-sync-targets`.

## Positions

- Canonical searchable rule entries: `implemented` at `.rule/rules/` and `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/store/store_query.rs`.
- Generated instruction target selection: `implemented` at `rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml`; ticket/spec/commit are description-only generated artifacts and token-efficiency retains narrow path globs.
- Minimal universal bootstrapper: `implemented` at `.agents/instructions/session-bootstrap.instructions.md` from canonical rule `89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855`.
- Session-side pinned-rule rendering: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`.
- CLI render surface: `implemented` at `memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs`.
- MCP render surface: `implemented` at `memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Exactly one generated bootstrapper instruction uses `applyTo: "**"`.
- The bootstrapper stays below 500 tokens and directs an agent to initialize/resume session context, search rules, pin relevant rule URNs, and render the pinned instruction set.
- `ticket-system.instructions.md`, `commit.instructions.md`, and `spec-system.instructions.md` remain deterministic generated artifacts with description-only discovery and no universal `applyTo`; `token-efficiency.instructions.md` retains only its narrow file globs.
- Their canonical `.rule` entries remain indexed and discoverable through representative rule searches.
- Session rendering accepts the current workspace session ID, resolves only pinned rule URNs from the sibling rule store, preserves canonical deterministic ordering, and returns generated markdown containing only those rule bodies.
- Non-rule pins never enter the rendered instruction set. Missing, malformed, cross-workspace, or non-rule references fail explicitly rather than broadening the render.
- Rule target generation remains deterministic and `rule sync-targets --check` passes.

# Non-goals

- Changing the canonical rule manifest or search index formats.
- Automatically selecting rules from vague semantic matches.
- Rendering ticket or spec bodies into the session instruction set.
- Removing narrowly scoped instruction files for concrete code paths.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. The generated always-on instruction set contains only the sub-500-token bootstrapper; the four large bodies are absent from that set.
2. Representative searches find canonical ticket-state, commit-hook, spec-authoring, and token-efficiency rule entries.
3. Spec-system guidance appears exactly once in its generated on-demand artifact.
4. `rule sync-targets --check` is deterministic on the reduced universal set.
5. Given pinned rule entries plus unrelated pins, session rendering emits exactly the pinned rule bodies in deterministic order.
6. Missing or invalid pinned rule references fail explicitly without rendering unrelated content.
7. Session CLI and MCP expose the focused render operation consistently.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Implementation ticket: [b4a8dc5e Minimal bootstrapper + selective instruction loading](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6/ticket.toml).
- Pin/view dependency: `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.
- Design dependency: `afa00b5c-c736-4d75-b157-d3e9ce90d819`.
- Selective render evidence: [exec-val-session-selective-rule-render-20260714](.test/default/executions/exec-val-session-selective-rule-render-20260714.json).
- Generation/discovery evidence: [exec-val-rule-selective-instruction-generation-20260714](.test/default/executions/exec-val-rule-selective-instruction-generation-20260714.json).
- Deterministic sync evidence: [exec-val-rule-sync-targets-20260714](.test/default/executions/exec-val-rule-sync-targets-20260714.json).

data_json.content: All three executions are now durable test-api evidence. I’m updating the owning spec’s stale positions and traceability so it names the implemented code paths, exact ticket manifest, and passing evidence artifacts before the ticket moves to review.

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Exactly one generated bootstrapper instruction uses `applyTo: "**"`.
- The bootstrapper stays below 500 tokens and directs an agent to initialize/resume session context, search rules, pin relevant rule URNs, and render the pinned instruction set.
- `ticket-system.instructions.md`, `commit.instructions.md`, and `spec-system.instructions.md` remain deterministic generated artifacts with description-only discovery and no universal `applyTo`; `token-efficiency.instructions.md` retains only its narrow file globs.
- Their canonical `.rule` entries remain indexed and discoverable through representative rule searches.
- Session rendering accepts the current workspace session ID, resolves only pinned rule URNs from the sibling rule store, preserves canonical deterministic ordering, and returns generated markdown containing only those rule bodies.
- Non-rule pins never enter the rendered instruction set. Missing, malformed, cross-workspace, or non-rule references fail explicitly rather than broadening the render.
- Rule target generation remains deterministic and `rule sync-targets --check` passes.

# Non-goals

- Changing the canonical rule manifest or search index formats.
- Automatically selecting rules from vague semantic matches.
- Rendering ticket or spec bodies into the session instruction set.
- Removing narrowly scoped instruction files for concrete code paths.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. The generated always-on instruction set contains only the sub-500-token bootstrapper; the four large bodies are absent from that set.
2. Representative searches find canonical ticket-state, commit-hook, spec-authoring, and token-efficiency rule entries.
3. Spec-system guidance appears exactly once in its generated on-demand artifact.
4. `rule sync-targets --check` is deterministic on the reduced universal set.
5. Given pinned rule entries plus unrelated pins, session rendering emits exactly the pinned rule bodies in deterministic order.
6. Missing or invalid pinned rule references fail explicitly without rendering unrelated content.
7. Session CLI and MCP expose the focused render operation consistently.

# Traceability

arguments.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Replace the always-on generated instruction load with one minimal bootstrapper plus discoverable, pinnable rule entries that are rendered into a focused per-session instruction set.

## Motivation ("why")

Universal `applyTo: "**"` instruction files inject unrelated ticket, spec, commit, and token-efficiency guidance into every task. The canonical rule store already makes those bodies searchable; sessions need a small discovery contract and an explicit render path instead of unconditional expansion.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on only the minimal bootstrapper being universal, the large instruction bodies remaining searchable and available on demand, and a session rendering exactly its pinned rule entries without expanding unrelated rules.

## Guards

- `val-session-selective-rule-render`.
- `val-rule-selective-instruction-generation`.
- `val-rule-sync-targets`.

## Positions

- Canonical searchable rule entries: `implemented` at `.rule/rules/` and `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/store/store_query.rs`.
- Generated instruction target selection: `implemented` at `rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml`; ticket/spec/commit are description-only generated artifacts and token-efficiency retains narrow path globs.
- Minimal universal bootstrapper: `implemented` at `.agents/instructions/session-bootstrap.instructions.md` from canonical rule `89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855`.
- Session-side pinned-rule rendering: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`.
- CLI render surface: `implemented` at `memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs`.
- MCP render surface: `implemented` at `memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Exactly one generated bootstrapper instruction uses `applyTo: "**"`.
- The bootstrapper stays below 500 tokens and directs an agent to initialize/resume session context, search rules, pin relevant rule URNs, and render the pinned instruction set.
- `ticket-system.instructions.md`, `commit.instructions.md`, and `spec-system.instructions.md` remain deterministic generated artifacts with description-only discovery and no universal `applyTo`; `token-efficiency.instructions.md` retains only its narrow file globs.
- Their canonical `.rule` entries remain indexed and discoverable through representative rule searches.
- Session rendering accepts the current workspace session ID, resolves only pinned rule URNs from the sibling rule store, preserves canonical deterministic ordering, and returns generated markdown containing only those rule bodies.
- Non-rule pins never enter the rendered instruction set. Missing, malformed, cross-workspace, or non-rule references fail explicitly rather than broadening the render.
- Rule target generation remains deterministic and `rule sync-targets --check` passes.

# Non-goals

- Changing the canonical rule manifest or search index formats.
- Automatically selecting rules from vague semantic matches.
- Rendering ticket or spec bodies into the session instruction set.
- Removing narrowly scoped instruction files for concrete code paths.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. The generated always-on instruction set contains only the sub-500-token bootstrapper; the four large bodies are absent from that set.
2. Representative searches find canonical ticket-state, commit-hook, spec-authoring, and token-efficiency rule entries.
3. Spec-system guidance appears exactly once in its generated on-demand artifact.
4. `rule sync-targets --check` is deterministic on the reduced universal set.
5. Given pinned rule entries plus unrelated pins, session rendering emits exactly the pinned rule bodies in deterministic order.
6. Missing or invalid pinned rule references fail explicitly without rendering unrelated content.
7. Session CLI and MCP expose the focused render operation consistently.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Implementation ticket: [b4a8dc5e Minimal bootstrapper + selective instruction loading](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6/ticket.toml).
- Pin/view dependency: `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.
- Design dependency: `afa00b5c-c736-4d75-b157-d3e9ce90d819`.
- Selective render evidence: [exec-val-session-selective-rule-render-20260714](.test/default/executions/exec-val-session-selective-rule-render-20260714.json).
- Generation/discovery evidence: [exec-val-rule-selective-instruction-generation-20260714](.test/default/executions/exec-val-rule-selective-instruction-generation-20260714.json).
- Deterministic sync evidence: [exec-val-rule-sync-targets-20260714](.test/default/executions/exec-val-rule-sync-targets-20260714.json).

field_map.implementation_summary: Implemented selective instruction loading end to end. Added one sub-500-token universal session bootstrap rule and generated instruction; removed universal applyTo from ticket, commit, and spec guidance while preserving description-only generated artifacts and narrow token-efficiency globs; removed duplicated spec-system content at its canonical frontmatter rule. Added deterministic pinned-rule rendering in session-api with non-rule exclusion and explicit missing/cross-workspace failures, plus render-instructions CLI and session_runtime_render_instructions MCP surfaces with execution-level tests.
field_map.validation_summary: Passing evidence recorded in test-api: exec-val-session-selective-rule-render-20260714 (129 session API/CLI/MCP tests), exec-val-rule-selective-instruction-generation-20260714 (50 rule-cli tests plus global universal-glob and representative discovery checks), and exec-val-rule-sync-targets-20260714 (status ok, no generated drift). Focused rustfmt check for session-api/session-cli/session-mcp passed.

# Action 8 Independent Review Handoff

Resume at the repository root. The durable session remediation is closed, and selective instruction loading is implemented, validated, and awaiting independent review. No commit was created. Review the current working tree without disturbing unrelated feedback, viewer, or session-capture changes.

## Durable Session Identity

- `workspace_session_id`: `448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0`
- outgoing `run_id`: `8782c98d-1489-4550-9ea5-c799cb87e6f1`
- `handoff_id`: `45e5771e-4029-45d1-b9c7-b8a0ec597141`
- persisted record: [45e5771e persisted action-8 review handoff](./.session/runtime/workspaces/448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0/handoffs/45e5771e-4029-45d1-b9c7-b8a0ec597141.json)
- runtime context, schema version 1: [448a0119 durable workspace context](./.session/runtime/workspaces/448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0/context.json)
- exact resume command:

  `session-cli resume --workspace-session-id 448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0 --predecessor-run-id 8782c98d-1489-4550-9ea5-c799cb87e6f1`

The persisted workflow still stores its `action-8` node as `pending`, but its live ticket resolution is `in-review`. Treat the ticket store as authoritative.

## Long-Horizon Goal

Ticket UUID `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`:

[effba966 Dynamic session bootstrapping & context routing redesign](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8/ticket.toml)

The goal is durable session identity and focused context loading without injecting unrelated rule bodies into every model request.

## Current Ticket Track

Ticket UUID `6b1edff1-bc32-40c7-b3a9-fb1292b0213f`:

[6b1edff1 Authoritative finish, live ticket state, atomic durability, and CLI contract](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b1edff1-bc32-40c7-b3a9-fb1292b0213f/ticket.toml)

State: `done`. Its lock ownership, finished-init immutability, race coverage, and durability-claim remediation passed independent review.

Ticket UUID `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6`:

[b4a8dc5e Minimal bootstrapper + selective instruction loading](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6/ticket.toml)

State: `in-review`. Implementation and validation summaries are current. This is the only active review target.

## Owning Contract

Spec UUID `a28a88db-2b41-49d2-897c-6dbcdb313255`:

[a28a88db Minimal bootstrapper and selective rule loading](./.spec/specs/a28a88db-2b41-49d2-897c-6dbcdb313255/spec.toml)

The spec is aligned-structure v2 and marks the bootstrapper, target selection, session renderer, CLI, and MCP positions implemented.

## Confirmed Implementation

- Exactly one generated universal instruction remains:
  [session-bootstrap.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/session-bootstrap.instructions.md).
- Canonical bootstrap rule UUID `89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855`:
  [89330b3b Session Bootstrap](./.rule/rules/89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855/rule.toml).
- Ticket, commit, and spec guidance remain generated and searchable but no longer use universal `applyTo`.
- Duplicated spec-system guidance was removed at its canonical rule.
- [store.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs) resolves only pinned rule URNs, excludes non-rule pins, orders deterministically, and fails explicitly for missing or cross-workspace rules.
- [store_tests.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs) covers focused ordering and missing-rule failure.
- [session CLI](./memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs) exposes `render-instructions` with parser and real-dispatch tests.
- [session MCP](./memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs) exposes `session_runtime_render_instructions`.
- [rule target configuration](./rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml) generates the bootstrapper deterministically.
- The current CLI and MCP manifests contain the intended test-only `rule-api` dependency and are LF-normalized:
  [session-cli Cargo.toml](./memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/Cargo.toml) and [session-mcp Cargo.toml](./memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/Cargo.toml).

## Validation Evidence

- [exec-val-session-selective-rule-render-20260714](./.test/default/executions/exec-val-session-selective-rule-render-20260714.json): 129 tests passed across session API, CLI, and MCP.
- [exec-val-rule-selective-instruction-generation-20260714](./.test/default/executions/exec-val-rule-selective-instruction-generation-20260714.json): 50 rule-cli tests passed; exactly one universal generated instruction was found.
- [exec-val-rule-sync-targets-20260714](./.test/default/executions/exec-val-rule-sync-targets-20260714.json): target synchronization returned `status: ok` with no changed outputs.
- A final CLI/MCP rerun passed 21 tests across seven suites.
- Focused formatting, diagnostics, ticket/spec health, and scoped `git diff --check` passed.

The persisted handoff lists the three action-8 validation gates as required but does not inline their outcomes. Use the linked test-api executions above as authoritative evidence.

## Decisions To Preserve

- Keep only the sub-500-token bootstrapper universal.
- Preserve large guidance as canonical searchable rules and deterministic on-demand generated artifacts.
- Render only explicitly pinned rule URNs; never broaden on missing, malformed, non-rule, or cross-workspace references.
- Keep ordering deterministic by canonical rule order and slug.
- Keep API, CLI, and MCP behavior consistent.
- Do not reintroduce duplicated spec-system content.
- Do not hand-edit generated instruction files; update canonical rules and run target synchronization.
- Do not stage, revert, or commit unrelated workspace changes.

## Remaining Blocker

Independent review is the sole blocker. No known implementation or validation defect remains.

The working tree contains unrelated feedback-ticket, viewer, historical test-evidence, and live session-capture changes. Review only the action-8 files and do not treat unrelated deletions or dirt as part of this ticket.

## Next Actions

1. Resume using the exact command above.
2. Read [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md), the action-8 ticket, owning spec, three validation executions, and the current action-8 diff.
3. Independently review rule resolution, deterministic ordering, fail-closed behavior, CLI/MCP parity, generated target selection, and spec traceability.
4. First validation check: run `rtk cargo test -p session-api -p session-cli -p session-mcp`.
5. Run `rtk cargo test -p rule-cli`.
6. Run `rtk cargo run -p rule-cli --bin rule -- sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml --check`.
7. Confirm the generated instruction scan still finds only [session-bootstrap.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/session-bootstrap.instructions.md) with `applyTo: "**"`.
8. Confirm focused formatting and scoped `git diff --check`, including both current Cargo manifests.
9. If review finds no blocker, close ticket UUID `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6` from `in-review` to `done`, update the durable workflow if appropriate, and release any board lease.
10. Create a commit only if explicitly requested.

Execute these actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

## Completion Status

- Durable remediation closure: `completed`.
- Minimal universal bootstrapper: `completed`.
- Selective session API rendering: `completed`.
- CLI and MCP render transports: `completed`.
- Generated-target and discovery validation: `completed`.
- Test-api evidence persistence: `completed`.
- Ticket/spec reconciliation: `completed`.
- Independent action-8 review: `not-started`.
- Action-8 ticket closure: `blocked` on independent review.
- Commit: `not-started`; not requested.

## Definition Of Done

- Independent review reports no blocking correctness, generation, transport, or traceability finding.
- Current-tree session package, rule-cli, synchronization, formatting, and whitespace checks pass.
- Exactly one universal generated instruction remains.
- All three test-api executions remain retrievable and linked to ticket UUID `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6` and spec UUID `a28a88db-2b41-49d2-897c-6dbcdb313255`.
- Ticket UUID `b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6` reaches `done`.
- Board ownership is released.
- Unrelated workspace changes remain untouched.

## Board And Session Notes

The ticket board is clear: zero active entries, stale entries, conflicts, or file ownership claims.

The current Copilot capture metadata is available at [14f59cf5 session capture](./.session/sessions/14f59cf5-2d61-46aa-b90d-625fb9dd3029/session.json). Treat capture history as diagnostic evidence only; the ticket, spec, test executions, runtime context, and persisted handoff are the authoritative restart sources.

No commit was created during action 8.
